quote: across the full chain of the Kuril Islands
issue: Inconsistent spelling: 'Kuril' here vs 'Kurile' used throughout the rest of the chapter (lines 124, 131, 181, 210, 215, 241).
suggestion: Change to 'Kurile Islands' for consistency, or adopt one spelling globally.

quote: ‹contested as to the interpretation of Ainu agency and objectives›
issue: The epistemic tag contains extra explanatory text not in the standard format (‹contested›). This appears to be a pipeline note or editorial comment that leaked into the prose.
suggestion: Remove the extra text and use either just ‹contested› or a natural-language aside.

quote: the Handbook's chapter on language contact <Ref k="shiraishitangiku2022" />
issue: Cross-reference written in prose ('the Handbook's chapter') instead of using the <Xr> component, which is the standard method used elsewhere (e.g., line 61, 274). This may be a leftover from an earlier draft or refers to an external volume; either way it is inconsistent with the rest of the chapter.
suggestion: Replace with an <Xr> link if the chapter is part of this grammar; otherwise clarify what 'Handbook' refers to.

issue: The gloss 'prosaic folktale' uses 'prosaic' which normally means 'dull' or 'commonplace'; the intended meaning is likely 'prose folktale' (as opposed to verse epic). This is either a glossing mistake or an infelicitous word choice.
suggestion: Change gloss to 'prose folktale' if that is the intended meaning.

quote: including the Matsumae no koto — Ezo kotoba no koto of c. 1600 with 117 words compiled for use by merchants
issue: The em dash between 'Matsumae no koto' and 'Ezo kotoba no koto' is unclear; it might be intended as a parenthetical remark or an alternative title. The punctuation is awkward and may confuse the reader.
suggestion: Consider using parentheses: 'including the Matsumae no koto (Ezo kotoba no koto) of c. 1600' or rephrase to clarify the relationship between the two titles.

quote: establishes support mechanisms for cultural and language activities, and authorized the establishment
issue: Tense inconsistency: 'establishes' (present) is followed by 'authorized' (past) within the same list of actions of the 2019 act. The verb should be present tense 'authorizes' for consistency.
